# ARCHITECTURE.md

CVX — Distributed HPC Vision AI Platform

---

# 1. Overview

CVX is a distributed, high-performance Vision AI platform designed for:

* Petabyte-scale storage
* Terabyte-scale memory
* Multi-node GPU clusters (H100-class hardware)
* Streaming and batch processing
* Agentic multimodal reasoning
* Distributed training and serving

The system is modular, memory-aware, storage-agnostic, and designed for
large-scale production deployments.

---

# 2. High-Level Architecture

## 2.1 Layered Logical Architecture (Mermaid)

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
    A[Application Layer]
    B[Agentic Reasoning Layer]
    C[Model Serving Layer]
    D[Distributed Compute Layer]
    E[Streaming + Batch Layer]
    F[Memory + Cache Layer]
    G[Storage Abstraction Layer]

    A --> B
    B --> C
    C --> D
    D --> E
    E --> F
    F --> G
```

---

# 3. Repository Structure

```
cvx/
├── docker/
├── k8s/
├── benchmarks/
├── tests/
├── pyproject.toml
└── cvx/
    ├── core/
    ├── memory/
    ├── cache/
    ├── storage/
    ├── models/
    ├── image/
    ├── video/
    ├── multimodal/
    ├── agent/
    ├── features/
    ├── search/
    ├── anomaly/
    ├── streaming/
    ├── distributed/
    ├── serving/
    ├── edge/
    ├── training/
    ├── experiments/
    ├── reporting/
    └── api/
```

The outer `cvx/` is the repository root.
The inner `cvx/` is the Python package.

---

# 4. Memory and Cache Architecture

## 4.1 Data Flow (Mermaid)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Object Storage]
    B[NVMe Cache]
    C[Memory-Mapped Dataset]
    D[Ray Object Store]
    E[Pinned CPU Memory]
    F[GPU HBM Memory]
    G[CUDA Streams]

    A --> B
    B --> C
    C --> D
    D --> E
    E --> F
    F --> G
```

---

## 4.2 Memory Hierarchy (SVG)

```svg
<svg width="600" height="300" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect x="50" y="20" width="500" height="40" fill="#f2f2f2" stroke="#000"/>
  <text x="300" y="45" text-anchor="middle">Object Storage (PB)</text>

  <rect x="50" y="70" width="500" height="40" fill="#e6e6e6" stroke="#000"/>
  <text x="300" y="95" text-anchor="middle">NVMe Cache</text>

  <rect x="50" y="120" width="500" height="40" fill="#d9d9d9" stroke="#000"/>
  <text x="300" y="145" text-anchor="middle">RAM (Memory-Mapped)</text>

  <rect x="50" y="170" width="500" height="40" fill="#cccccc" stroke="#000"/>
  <text x="300" y="195" text-anchor="middle">Pinned CPU Memory</text>

  <rect x="50" y="220" width="500" height="40" fill="#bfbfbf" stroke="#000"/>
  <text x="300" y="245" text-anchor="middle">GPU HBM (H100)</text>
</svg>
```

---

# 5. Distributed Compute Architecture

## 5.1 Ray + Spark Integration (Mermaid)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[Kafka]
    B[Ray Cluster]
    C[Spark Cluster]
    D[GPU Workers]
    E[Model Serving]

    A --> B
    B --> D
    C --> B
    D --> E
```

Responsibilities:

* Ray: training, serving, GPU scheduling
* Spark: feature engineering, ETL
* Kafka: streaming ingestion

---

# 6. Multi-GPU Serving

## 6.1 Serving Flow (Mermaid)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[Client Request]
    B[Load Balancer]
    C[Dynamic Batching]
    D[CUDA Graph Execution]
    E[Multi-GPU Inference]
    F[Response]

    A --> B --> C --> D --> E --> F
```

Capabilities:

* CUDA Graph capture
* Tensor parallel execution
* FP8/BF16 precision
* NVLink topology awareness

---

# 7. Agentic Vision Architecture

## 7.1 Agent Reasoning Loop (Mermaid)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Input]
    B[Perception]
    C[Memory Graph]
    D[Tool Router]
    E[Reasoning Loop]
    F[Action Execution]

    A --> B
    B --> C
    C --> D
    D --> E
    E --> F
```

Capabilities:

* Scene understanding
* Retrieval-augmented reasoning
* Multi-step decision making
* Tool orchestration

---

# 8. Streaming Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[Kafka Producer]
    B[Kafka Broker]
    C[Microbatch Processor]
    D[Inference Pipeline]
    E[Feature Store]

    A --> B --> C --> D --> E
```

Supports:

* Backpressure handling
* Event-driven inference
* Streaming feature updates

---

# 9. Storage Abstraction

All storage backends implement a common interface:

* Local filesystem
* S3-compatible storage
* GCS
* Azure Blob
* OpenSearch
* Feature stores

This ensures portability across cloud and on-prem deployments.

---

# 10. Testing Strategy

```
tests/
├── unit/
├── integration/
├── distributed/
├── memory/
└── performance/
```

Testing domains:

* API validation
* Distributed coordination
* Memory stability
* GPU throughput
* Streaming latency

---

# 11. Deployment Topologies

## 11.1 Development

* Single-node
* Docker Compose
* Mock storage

## 11.2 Staging

* Small Ray cluster
* Kafka single broker
* Multi-GPU node

## 11.3 Production

* Multi-node Ray cluster
* Kafka cluster
* Spark cluster
* Distributed object storage
* H100 GPU nodes

---

# 12. Scalability Model

| Dimension | Capability                 |
| --------- | -------------------------- |
| Storage   | Petabyte-scale             |
| Memory    | TB RAM + GPU HBM           |
| Compute   | Horizontal Ray scaling     |
| Serving   | Multi-GPU dynamic batching |
| Streaming | Kafka-based real-time      |
| Training  | Distributed multi-node     |

---

# 13. Design Guarantees

* Modular layer isolation
* Memory-aware data flow
* Storage abstraction
* Distributed-first architecture
* GPU-optimized execution
* Agentic multimodal extensibility

---

# 14. Summary

CVX is designed as a distributed AI platform capable of handling:

* Large-scale storage
* High-throughput GPU inference
* Continuous training
* Real-time streaming
* Agentic multimodal reasoning

The architecture ensures extensibility, scalability, and hardware efficiency
across cloud, on-prem, and edge environments.

:::
